items.title: Customer segmentation without labels
items.scenario: A subscription company has purchase frequency, session length, and support tickets per user—but no predefined 'persona' labels. Unsupervised clustering groups users into segments such as power users, churn risks, and occasional browsers.
items.explanation: Cluster assignments become actionable marketing segments. The algorithm discovered structure; product teams later name and validate segments with business knowledge.

items.title: Compressing sensor readings for monitoring
items.scenario: A factory records 200 sensor channels every minute. Dimensionality reduction finds a 5-component summary that captures most variation, plotted on a dashboard for operators.
items.explanation: Operators spot drift or abnormal joint patterns without inspecting all 200 raw channels. The low-dimensional view is an unsupervised structural summary.

items.title: Fraud screening with no labeled fraud cases yet
items.scenario: A new payment product has millions of transactions but few confirmed fraud labels. Anomaly detection flags transactions far from the learned 'normal' density for analyst review.
items.explanation: Unsupervised scoring prioritizes investigation queues before enough labeled fraud exists to train a supervised classifier.
